title: LTX-2.3 i2v OOM on cloud 4090 (24GB) - GGUF BF16 dequant spike (v0.27)
description:
⚠️ APPROACH SUPERSEDED (2026-07-04, MPI-187): the 24GB-GGUF hardening hunt below (--vram-headroom, lower quant, streamed/block-wise dequant to survive the dequantize_blocks_BF16 spike) is largely MOOT. Root fix is upstream: MPI-189 (cu130 Pod image) + MPI-190 (revert Pod transformer GGUF->bf16). No GGUF = no dequant upcast = the OOM's mechanism is GONE, not worked-around. cu130 also makes bf16 cheap to fault (~11s vs the old ~5min that forced GGUF in the first place, MPI-168). So this card should CLOSE via MPI-190 once a cu130 Pod live-confirms bf16 LTX fits + faults fast on a 32GB card — NOT via 24GB-GGUF flag-tuning. The sub-issues (2 TAESD-preview-None, 3 perf, 4 gallery blob-404) are INDEPENDENT of cu130 and stay tracked here or split out. Keep --vram-headroom knowledge as fallback ONLY if a <32GB tier must keep GGUF. Original investigation preserved below. ---

INVESTIGATION (found 2026-07-03, live v0.27 verify on RunPod 4090 / v0.11.0-cu124). Separate from MPI-148 (the v0.27 bump shipped clean - Wan 5B + PiD gen'd OK on the same Pod). Pod-side logs (RunPod console) gave the REAL stack, sharper than the app-side error.

=== 1) LTX-2.3 i2v OOM - ROOT CAUSE = GGUF BF16 dequant spike, NOT the sampler ===
torch.OutOfMemoryError: device limit 23.64 GiB, allocated 22.53 GiB, requested +576 MiB, CUDA free 22.81 MiB -> 'Got an OOM, unloading all loaded models'. Pod traceback pins the failing op:
  File '/workspace/comfyui/custom_nodes/ComfyUI-GGUF/dequant.py', line 62, in dequantize_blocks_BF16
    return (blocks.view(torch.int16).to(torch.int32) << 16).view(torch.float32)
So the OOM is in the GGUF node UPCASTING the quantized transformer (int16->int32->float32) at sample time - a ~2x transient memory spike. Context leading in:
  - '[INFO] loaded partially; 21758 MB usable, 21736.71 MB loaded, 231.26 MB offloaded, lowvram patches: 0' -> the transformer already nearly fills 24GB under lowvram/aimdo BEFORE sampling.
  - Then dequantize_blocks_BF16 needs +576MB transient -> tips past 23.64 GiB -> OOM.
MECHANISM: the balanced-Pod LTX path ships the GGUF (Q8) transformer (MPI-168). GGUF saves DISK/load VRAM but dequant materializes a wider dtype at runtime; on a 24GB card already packed, the dequant spike has no headroom. This likely worked (or was untested at this res) on v0.26; whether v0.27's int8/aimdo changes shifted the baseline is UNKNOWN.
OPEN QUESTIONS / NEXT STEPS:
  a. Is this res/tier-specific? Log the exact i2v tier + resolution ('i2v_ms' = medium-smooth?) and retry a lower tier to find the 24GB ceiling.
  b. Does v0.26 OOM at the same config? (regression-vs-inherent test).
  c. Can the GGUF path avoid the full-tensor BF16 dequant spike (block-wise / streamed dequant), or should the 24GB/broad-tier Pod use the BF16 (non-GGUF) transformer that skips dequant, or a smaller quant? Cross-ref docs/builder/research/quant-and-coldstart-investigation.md (Q8_0 vs NVFP4) + lora-merge-ltx.md (extract-from-BF16 note).
  d. aimdo/dynamic-vram is active ('loaded partially ... lowvram') - test --reserve-vram or --disable-dynamic-vram, and whether reserving headroom for the dequant spike prevents the OOM.

=== 2) TAESD preview path broken on Pod ===
'Warning: TAESD previews enabled, but could not find models/vae_approx/None'. The preview VAE resolves to a literal 'None' filename. Cross-ref the LTX-preview-cannot-use-taesd finding (project memory) - LTX packed latent can't use taesd; the Pod may be mis-enabling it. Minor but noisy; confirm the Pod's --preview-method / vae_approx wiring for LTX.

=== 3) Perf: cloud 4090 slow ===
LTX ~8 min click->about-to-generate (then OOM); Wan 5B ~2 min gen. User LOCAL: Wan ~20s + ~1min warm. Cloud 4090 far slower. Timeline (app.log): 21:16:08 Pod create -> 21:26:55 first history (~11min boot + 40GB LTX weight load from network volume) -> 21:39:03 OOM. Likely boot+load not gen. Fold datapoint into docs/builder/research/pod-perf-investigation.md (already logs 'cloud 4090 not faster than local 4060Ti', open suspects aimdo overhead + host P-State throttle).

=== 4) App gallery blob 404 flood ===
Repeated 'Failed to load resource: net::ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND' on 'blob:http://127.0.0.1:3000/<uuid>/...' rendering the gallery (Sidecar test, 95 assets). Blob URLs 404 - likely a revoke race or a blob outliving its asset. Separate app/renderer bug (cosmetic-ish). grep createObjectURL / revokeObjectURL in the gallery/history render path.

SEVERITY: HIGH for (1) - if LTX i2v OOMs on a 24GB cloud 4090 (the broad-tier Pod card), the balanced LTX offering is broken for that tier. (2)/(3)/(4) lower. Split into sub-cards if they diverge. BRANCH: RunPod.
activeSessionTitle: MPI-185 LTX GGUF dequant OOM investigation
